item.title: Maine’s race to the top on taxes
item.description: After opposing similar tax hikes in her state, Janet Mills is supporting a millionaires tax for Maine as she runs against Graham Platner in the Senate primary.

item.title: America’s silent guardians
item.description: Reflecting on four decades in the U.S. intelligence community and its quietly extraordinary work.

item.title: U.S. cut aid to Africa. The continent proved resilient.
item.description: Trump’s large aid cuts to Africa were predicted to lead to catastrophe. Instead, the cuts may have helped Africa find its economic independence.

item.title: Hegseth’s firing of a top general is the latest sign of Pentagon turmoil
item.description: The defense secretary is better at waging culture wars than the Iran war.

item.title: The environmental rules that caused an environmental disaster
item.description: The Potomac sewage spill might have been averted if not for federal bureaucracy.

item.title: Never subsidize a sports stadium. And definitely not like this.
Coming out as Christian on Easter
item.description: Resurrection and reconstruction, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Mr. President, let’s make a deal on tariffs
item.description: Tariff refunds, small businesses and distracted parenting, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: New data from Argentina shows the real answer to poverty
item.description: The poverty rate fell to 28 percent from 53 percent over two years.

item.title: Trump’s Iran own goal
item.description: The war has empowered Iranian hard-liners, blocked a vital shipping lane and handed a windfall to Russia.

item.title: The verdict against Meta and Google carries sinister implications
item.description: Diluting democracy’s foundational belief in individual agency opens the door to governmental overreach.

item.title: Congress is talking to Putin’s enablers
item.description: Lawmakers should build ties with Russia’s democratic forces.

item.title: Will the next William Henry Harrison please stand up?
item.description: A candidate in the mold of our ninth and short-serving president is the perfect response to Trump.

item.title: Easter destroys the ultimate weapon of tyranny
item.description: In proclaiming the resurrection of Jesus, Easter denies the power of death.

item.title: ‘Israel made us do it’: The Democratic Party has taken a turn
item.description: The party’s 2028 campaign is shaping up as one unprecedentedly hostile to the Jewish state.

item.title: NATO is refusing to help Trump with his Iran fiasco. Cue the tantrums.
item.description: Iran war compels America’s oldest allies to turn their backs on the president.
